# Database Schema Reference

## Tables

### crawled_profile
  - `linkedin_url` (VARCHAR(500), not null) ‚Äî Full LinkedIn URL
  - `public_identifier` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî LinkedIn public identifier token
  - `first_name` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî First name
  - `last_name` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî Last name
  - `full_name` (VARCHAR(500), nullable) ‚Äî Full name
  - `headline` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Profile headline
  - `about` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Profile about section
  - `location_city` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî City location
  - `location_state` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî State/province location
  - `location_country` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî Country location
  - `location_country_code` (VARCHAR(10), nullable) ‚Äî ISO country code
  - `location_raw` (VARCHAR(500), nullable) ‚Äî Raw location string
  - `connections_count` (INTEGER, nullable) ‚Äî Total number of connections
  - `follower_count` (INTEGER, nullable) ‚Äî Total number of followers
  - `open_to_work` (BOOLEAN, nullable) ‚Äî Is the profile open to work
  - `premium` (BOOLEAN, nullable) ‚Äî Is it a premium account
  - `current_company_name` (VARCHAR(500), nullable) ‚Äî Name of the current company
  - `current_position` (VARCHAR(500), nullable) ‚Äî Current job title/position
  - `company_id` (VARCHAR, nullable) ‚Äî Resolved internal company ID
  - `seniority_level` (VARCHAR(100), nullable) ‚Äî Deduced seniority level
  - `function_area` (VARCHAR(100), nullable) ‚Äî Deduced function/department area
  - `embedding_openai` (VECTOR(1536), nullable) ‚Äî OpenAI text-embedding-3-small vector
  - `embedding_nomic` (VECTOR(768), nullable) ‚Äî nomic-embed-text-v1.5 vector
  - `embedding_model` (VARCHAR(64), nullable) ‚Äî Model that generated the active embedding
  - `embedding_dim` (SMALLINT, nullable) ‚Äî Dimension of the active embedding
  - `embedding_updated_at` (DATETIME, nullable) ‚Äî When embedding was last generated
  - `search_vector` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Text search vector
  - `source_app_user_id` (VARCHAR, nullable) ‚Äî User who initiated the crawl, if any
  - `data_source` (VARCHAR(50), not null) ‚Äî Source of data collection (e.g. extension, api)
  - `has_enriched_data` (BOOLEAN, not null) ‚Äî Whether extended enrichment has been applied
  - `last_crawled_at` (DATETIME, nullable) ‚Äî When this profile was last crawled/synced
  - `profile_image_url` (VARCHAR(1000), nullable) ‚Äî URL to profile image
  - `raw_profile` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Raw JSON payload of the profile data
  - `created_at` (DATETIME, not null)
  - `updated_at` (DATETIME, not null)
  - `deleted_at` (DATETIME, nullable)

### connection
  - `app_user_id` (VARCHAR, not null) ‚Äî User who owns this connection
  - `crawled_profile_id` (VARCHAR, not null) ‚Äî Profile representing the connected person
  - `connected_at` (DATE, nullable) ‚Äî When the connection was established
  - `emails` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Comma-separated email addresses
  - `phones` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Comma-separated phone numbers
  - `notes` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Personal notes about this connection
  - `tags` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Comma-separated structural tags
  - `sources` (ARRAY, nullable) ‚Äî Where this connection was imported from (e.g. LinkedIn)
  - `source_details` (TEXT, nullable) ‚Äî Raw JSON data from the import source
  - `affinity_score` (FLOAT, nullable) ‚Äî Calculated affinity score (0-100)
  - `dunbar_tier` (VARCHAR(50), nullable) ‚Äî Assigned Dunbar number tier
  - `affinity_source_count` (FLOAT, not null) ‚Äî Number of interaction sources factored into affinity
  - `affinity_recency` (FLOAT, not null) ‚Äî Recency dimension of affinity score
  - `affinity_career_overlap` (FLOAT, not null) ‚Äî Career overlap dimension of affinity score
  - `affinity_mutual_connections` (FLOAT, not null) ‚Äî Mutual connections dimension of affinity score
  - `affinity_external_contact` (FLOAT, not null) ‚Äî External contact warmth signal
  - `affinity_embedding_similarity` (FLOAT, not null) ‚Äî Embedding similarity signal
  - `affinity_computed_at` (DATETIME, nullable) ‚Äî When affinity was last calculated
  - `affinity_version` (INTEGER, not null) ‚Äî Version of affinity algorithm used
  - `tenant_id` (VARCHAR, not null)
  - `bu_id` (VARCHAR, not null)
  - `created_at` (DATETIME, not null)
  - `updated_at` (DATETIME, not null)
  - `deleted_at` (DATETIME, nullable)
  - `archived_at` (DATETIME, nullable)
  - `created_by` (VARCHAR, nullable)
  - `updated_by` (VARCHAR, nullable)
  - `is_active` (BOOLEAN, not null)
  - `version` (INTEGER, not null)
  - `source` (VARCHAR, nullable)
  - `id` (VARCHAR, not null)

### company
  - `canonical_name` (VARCHAR(255), not null) ‚Äî The globally recognized unique name for this company
  - `normalized_name` (VARCHAR(255), not null) ‚Äî Lowercase alphanumeric string for consistent fuzzy matching
  - `linkedin_url` (VARCHAR(500), nullable) ‚Äî Public LinkedIn company page URL
  - `universal_name` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî LinkedIn universal name representation
  - `website` (VARCHAR(500), nullable) ‚Äî Primary corporate website URL
  - `domain` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî Primary email domain for employees
  - `industry` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî High-level industry categorization
  - `founded_year` (INTEGER, nullable) ‚Äî Year the company was founded
  - `hq_city` (VARCHAR(255), nullable) ‚Äî City where headquarters is located
  - `hq_country` (VARCHAR(100), nullable) ‚Äî Country where headquarters is located
  - `employee_count_range` (VARCHAR(50), nullable) ‚Äî Bracket representation of employee size
  - `estimated_employee_count` (INTEGER, nullable) ‚Äî Estimated precise employee count
  - `size_tier` (VARCHAR(20), nullable) ‚Äî Internal categorization of company size (e.g. SMB, Enterprise)
  - `network_connection_count` (INTEGER, not null) ‚Äî Aggregated cache of known contacts connected to this company
  - `parent_company_id` (VARCHAR, nullable) ‚Äî Reference ID to an overarching corporate parent entity
  - `enrichment_sources` (ARRAY, nullable) ‚Äî List of third-party sources that contributed to this entity data
  - `enriched_at` (DATETIME, nullable) ‚Äî Timestamp of last external data enrichment
  - `pdl_id` (VARCHAR(100), nullable) ‚Äî People Data Labs company identifier
  - `wikidata_id` (VARCHAR(50), nullable) ‚Äî Wikidata Q-number identifier
  - `created_at` (DATETIME, not null)
  - `updated_at` (DATETIME, not null)
  - `deleted_at` (DATETIME, nullable)
  - `archived_at` (DATETIME, nullable)

  - `source` (VARCHAR, nullable)
  - `notes` (TEXT, nullable)
  - `id` (VARCHAR, not null)

## Business Rules

- The database is automatically scoped to the current user's network via Row-Level Security (RLS). No user ID filtering is needed in SQL queries.
- Always JOIN through `connection` to access profiles: `FROM crawled_profile cp JOIN connection c ON c.crawled_profile_id = cp.id`.
- All connections have a `crawled_profile` (stub or enriched). Check `cp.has_enriched_data` to distinguish.
- Stub profiles (`has_enriched_data = FALSE`) have basic CSV data (name, company, title) but no embedding, no about, no experience/education.
- Only profiles with a valid LinkedIn URL (`linkedin_url LIKE 'https://www.linkedin.com/in/%'`) can be enriched. Profiles from Google Contacts or other non-LinkedIn sources have `stub://` URLs and are not enrichable. When reporting enrichment stats, use the enrichable count as the denominator ‚Äî not total profiles.
- `experience` and `education` link to `crawled_profile` via `crawled_profile_id`.
- `company` links via `company_id` on both `crawled_profile` and `experience`.
- `role_alias` maps title variants to canonical titles with seniority_level and function_area.
- `funding_round` links to `company` via `company_id`. Contains round_type, amount_usd, lead_investors (text array), all_investors (text). NOT user-scoped (no RLS) ‚Äî public company data.
- `startup_tracking` links to `company` via `company_id` (1:1). Contains funding_stage, total_raised_usd, vertical, watching flag. NOT user-scoped.

## Required SELECT Columns

Every SQL query returning people MUST include these columns:

```sql
SELECT cp.id AS crawled_profile_id, c.id AS connection_id,
       cp.full_name, cp.headline, cp.current_position, cp.current_company_name,
       cp.location_city, cp.location_country, cp.linkedin_url, cp.public_identifier,
       cp.has_enriched_data,
       c.affinity_score, c.dunbar_tier, c.connected_at
```

Always alias `cp.id` as `crawled_profile_id` and `c.id` as `connection_id`.
Always include `cp.has_enriched_data` ‚Äî the UI uses this to show enriched cards vs. the "Enrich" button.

## Data Availability

- Some `company` metadata columns (industry, size_tier, estimated_employee_count) may be NULL. Use `resolve_company_aliases` tool for company lookups.
- ~79% of experience records have `seniority_level`; ~63% have `function_area`. Add ILIKE fallback on `cp.current_position` when filtering by these.
- `experience.start_date` is populated for ~98% of records. `experience.is_current` is TRUE for ~18% of records.
- Past roles have `is_current IS NULL` (not FALSE). Use `e.is_current IS NULL AND e.end_year IS NOT NULL` for "previously at" queries.
